mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance on modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for appropriate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the llm-d deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster named 'llm-d-cluster' with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Install llm-d
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> This command adds the llm-d Helm repository and updates the local Helm chart repository.
> This command installs the llm-d Helm chart in a new namespace called 'llm-d'.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the llm-d release from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the llm-d deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> This command backs up the current llm-d deployment configurations.
> This command upgrades the llm-d release to the specified version.
> This command verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 192.0.2.1:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install llm-d: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the llm-d Helm repository is not added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update' to add and update the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the llm-d pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node, or scale your cluster accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on llm-d pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the llm-d pods are failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
